Nutritatva

by Tanvi Khedkar
Clinical & Functional Nutritionist

Good nutrition is
more than
eating well.

We spend years learning mathematics, science, and history, but almost no time learning how to care for the one body we'll live in forever. Health isn't a subject most of us were taught; it's something we're often expected to figure out only after something goes wrong. If you're here, don't wait for a diagnosis to start understanding your body. Learning how food, lifestyle, and your daily choices shape your health may be the most valuable education you'll ever invest in.

Ready to understand what your body has been trying to tell you?
Book your first 30-minute consultation.

About

Why I practice this way.

🌿
Tanvi Khedkar
Clinical & Functional Nutritionist
MSc Clinical Nutrition Functional Nutritionist Symbiosis Institute

I've always been more interested in understanding the "why" behind a symptom than reaching for the quickest fix. When someone comes to me with fatigue, or hair fall, or bloating that won't go away, my first question isn't what to remove from the diet. It's what's actually going on.

Functional nutrition gave me a framework for that. It looks at the body as a whole, not as separate problems to be managed independently. Most of the time, what feels like three different issues turns out to have one common thread.

I work with Indian food and Indian lifestyles because that's the context most of my clients are actually living in. There's no point recommending foods you won't find at a local market or habits that don't fit a real day.

My approach is practical, evidence-based, and shaped around your specific situation. I don't believe in protocols that ignore the person following them.

PCOSThyroidGut Health DiabetesWeight Management Hormonal HealthFatigueCholesterol
Services

What I can help with.

Each service is built around your specific health picture. Nothing here is off the shelf.

🔍

Root Cause Nutrition Consultation

For people who want to understand why they haven't been feeling their best. We look at the whole picture, including your symptoms, lifestyle, and history, to find the underlying contributors rather than just addressing what's on the surface.

🦠

Gut Health Program

For bloating, acidity, IBS, constipation, and persistent indigestion. Poor digestion affects everything from your energy to your skin to your mood. This program works through the layers to find what's disrupting your gut and address it properly.

🌸

Hormone and PCOS Support

For hormonal imbalance, PCOS, irregular periods, and metabolic health concerns. Hormonal issues rarely have a single cause, so the approach here looks at multiple contributing factors, including blood sugar, stress, and gut health, together.

❤️

Heart Health and Cholesterol Management

For high cholesterol, elevated triglycerides, fatty liver, and cardiovascular risk. Dietary changes can have a meaningful impact on these markers, and this program is built around what the evidence actually supports, not generic low-fat advice.

⚖️

Weight Management

Sustainable fat loss and body recomposition without crash dieting. The focus here is on understanding what's been making weight loss difficult for you specifically, and building an approach that works with your body rather than against it.

🩸

Metabolic Health Program

For insulin resistance, diabetes, prediabetes, and improving long-term metabolic health. Blood sugar regulation affects nearly every system in the body. This program addresses it through targeted nutrition and practical lifestyle changes.

First Consultation

Not sure where to start?
That's what the first call is for.

The introductory consultation is a conversation, not a diagnosis. We talk about what's been going on, what you've already tried, and whether working together makes sense.

30 minutes · ₹499 · No obligation to continue

Your Journey

What your 3 months look like.

A structured, phased approach so your body heals in the right sequence and results actually stick. Click each phase to explore.

01 🔍 Week 1 to 2

Discovery and Deep Assessment

Root causes identified Functional blood analysis No more guessing

Your first consultation covers your complete health history, current symptoms, lifestyle, and food patterns. You get your blood work done and I analyse it using functional optimal ranges, not just lab normals. This is where we identify what is actually driving your symptoms.

📋
Full Health History ReviewWe go through your past diagnoses, medications, diet patterns, stress levels, and symptom timeline together. Nothing is too small to mention.
🩸
Functional Blood Report AnalysisI analyse your existing blood reports against functional optimal ranges (stricter than lab normals). Most clients discover imbalances that were hiding in plain sight.
🗺️
Root Cause MapBy the end of this phase, you have a clear picture of which body systems are involved and why, not a generic diagnosis, but a personalised explanation of your specific patterns.
02 🌿 Week 2 to 3

Your Protocol is Built

Indian food meal plan Supplement protocol 3-month roadmap

Based on your blood findings and root causes, I build your complete protocol. Everything is specific to you: your body, your food preferences, your lifestyle, and your budget for supplements.

🍱
Personalised Indian Food PlanReal meals using Indian ingredients. Dal, roti, sabzi, curd, specific combinations. Quantities, timings, and cooking methods that work for your body's specific needs.
💊
Targeted Supplement StackOnly what your blood markers show you actually need. Selected from clean-label Indian brands with good bioavailability. No guessing, no excess.
📅
3-Month RoadmapA clear phase-by-phase plan so you know exactly what to expect at each stage, including what we are working on, what symptoms to expect to improve, and when.
03 🌱 Month 1

Foundation Phase: Build and Stabilise

Energy improves Bloating reduces Cravings settle

The first month is about getting the foundations right. Gut healing, nutrient repletion, blood sugar stabilisation, and removing the key triggers keeping you symptomatic. Most clients notice meaningful changes within the first 2 to 3 weeks.

🔥
Gut Healing and Inflammation ControlWe remove inflammatory triggers and begin restoring gut lining integrity. This alone often resolves bloating, skin breakouts, and brain fog within 2 weeks.
⚡
Nutrient RepletionIron, B12, vitamin D, magnesium, depending on what your blood showed was depleted. Targeted nutrition and supplementation starts correcting these from day one.
📞
Weekly Check-in CallsWe review how your body is responding, adjust portions or timing, and address anything that comes up. You are never left to figure it out alone.
04 ⚡ Month 2

Correction Phase: Targeted Healing

Hormones stabilise Weight starts moving Sleep improves

With the foundation in place, we go deeper into the root cause. Hormonal rebalancing, metabolic correction, or gut repair, depending on which system is most involved. The protocol is adjusted based on how your body has been responding in month one.

🌸
Hormonal and Metabolic CorrectionFor PCOS, thyroid, and insulin resistance clients, this is the phase where blood markers begin shifting. We add or modify protocol elements based on your month 1 response.
🏋️
Body Composition FocusFor recomposition clients, we refine macros and meal timing based on your progress data. This is where fat loss and muscle building become more targeted and intentional.
📊
Mid-Program ReviewA detailed review of all symptoms, weight, energy, and available markers. We document progress, celebrate wins, and adjust the second half of the program accordingly.
05 🏆 Month 3

Integration Phase: Sustain and Thrive

Blood markers improve Sustainable habits Long-term plan ready

The goal of month 3 is to make your new healthy state your default. We retest key markers, confirm your improvements in the blood work, and transition you to a long-term maintenance plan you can follow independently and sustainably.

🔬
Re-testing and Results ConfirmationWe recheck the blood markers that were off at the start. Seeing the numbers move is one of the most motivating moments of the programme and confirms that the healing is real.
🗓️
Long-term Maintenance PlanA simplified version of your protocol designed for independent, sustainable use. You know what to eat, what to watch for, and when to re-check your markers.
🌿
Supplement Wind-downWhere appropriate, we reduce or discontinue supplements as food and lifestyle take over. The goal is always to need less, not more.
Interactive

Most symptoms have a common thread.

Select any condition to see what typically drives it and one food that can support the healing process.

👈Select a condition to understand what is usually driving it
🌸

PCOS

What's usually driving it
Insulin Resistance and Androgen Excess

PCOS is not primarily a hormonal problem. In most cases it starts with insulin resistance. When the body stops responding to insulin efficiently, the pancreas produces more of it, and that extra insulin signals the ovaries to produce androgens. That's what disrupts ovulation and causes the symptoms: irregular cycles, weight gain around the middle, acne, and hair thinning.

🌿 Supportive Food
🌱
Methi Seeds (Fenugreek)
Shown to improve insulin sensitivity and support more regular cycles when used consistently
🩸

Diabetes / Pre-Diabetes

What's usually driving it
Insulin Resistance and Beta Cell Fatigue

Type 2 diabetes doesn't appear overnight. It builds over years as the body gradually loses its ability to manage blood sugar efficiently. Inflammation, gut health, and overall dietary pattern all play a role. It's not simply about eating too much sugar. Catching it at the prediabetes stage gives a real opportunity to change course through food and lifestyle.

🌿 Supportive Food
🫘
Karela (Bitter Gourd)
Contains compounds that support glucose uptake and help reduce post-meal blood sugar spikes
🦋

Thyroid Dysfunction

What's usually driving it
Autoimmune Response and Nutrient Gaps

Most thyroid conditions, particularly hypothyroidism, are autoimmune in nature. The immune system is attacking thyroid tissue, often triggered by gut permeability, selenium deficiency, or iodine imbalance. Standard TSH testing often misses early-stage dysfunction. Nutrition can support immune regulation and thyroid hormone conversion when the right deficiencies are addressed.

🌿 Supportive Food
🥥
Brazil Nuts (2 per day)
One of the richest sources of selenium, which is needed to convert inactive T4 into active T3
🔥

Gut Issues

What's usually driving it
Microbial Imbalance and Compromised Gut Lining

Bloating, acidity, and irregular digestion are usually the result of an imbalance in the gut microbiome, reduced digestive enzyme activity, or a compromised gut lining. Antibiotics, chronic stress, and years of processed food are common disruptors. What makes gut issues particularly important is how far their effects reach, including skin, mood, immunity, and hormones are all affected.

🌿 Supportive Food
🥛
Homemade Curd (Dahi)
A natural source of live cultures that support microbial diversity and reduce bloating over time
💇

Hair Fall

What's usually driving it
Low Ferritin, Protein Insufficiency, or DHT Sensitivity

Hair loss is almost always a downstream symptom of something happening elsewhere in the body. Low ferritin (stored iron) is the most common nutritional driver, and it's frequently missed because haemoglobin looks normal. Protein intake, thyroid function, and DHT levels all play a role too. Addressing the actual cause usually makes more difference than any topical treatment.

🌿 Supportive Food
🌿
Til and Jaggery
Provides iron, zinc, and plant protein together, a combination that directly supports ferritin levels
⚡

Chronic Fatigue

What's usually driving it
B12, Iron, or Vitamin D Deficiency and Poor Cellular Energy

Persistent tiredness that sleep doesn't fix is usually a sign that something is off at the cellular level. B12 deficiency is particularly common in vegetarians and those on long-term medications. Low vitamin D and poor iron status are close behind. Once these are corrected through food and targeted supplementation, energy tends to improve fairly quickly.

🌿 Supportive Food
🥩
Rajma with Amla
Iron from rajma is absorbed significantly better when eaten alongside the vitamin C in amla
❤️

High Cholesterol

What's usually driving it
Liver Overload, Inflammation, and Insulin Resistance

The liver produces around 80% of the body's cholesterol. When blood sugar is dysregulated or inflammation is high, the liver produces more. This is why high cholesterol often appears alongside insulin resistance, even in people who eat carefully. Reducing carbohydrate load, managing inflammation, and supporting liver function tend to move the numbers more than simply cutting dietary fat.

🌿 Supportive Food
🧄
Raw Garlic (Lasun)
Allicin has been shown to reduce LDL, raise HDL, and lower triglycerides with consistent daily use
⚖️

Unexplained Weight Gain

What's usually driving it
Insulin Resistance, Cortisol Elevation, or Thyroid Slowdown

When someone eats carefully and still struggles with weight, there's nearly always a physiological reason. Insulin resistance causes the body to store glucose as fat rather than burn it. Elevated cortisol from chronic stress promotes abdominal fat storage. A sluggish thyroid reduces the metabolic rate. Understanding which of these is the primary driver changes the nutritional approach considerably.

🌿 Supportive Food
🫚
Cold-pressed Coconut Oil
MCTs provide quick energy without requiring insulin, and support thyroid function in those with sluggish metabolism
Real Results

Client Transformations.

These aren't before-and-after photos. They're changes in how people feel, what their blood work shows, and what they can do now that they couldn't before.

Blood Report Improvement 🩸

HbA1c down from 7.2 to 6.1

A 44-year-old client with Type 2 diabetes saw this change over three months through dietary adjustments and a structured meal timing approach. No medication changes were made.

Metabolic Health
Symptom Improvement 🌸

Regular cycles after 2 years of irregularity

A client with PCOS and significant insulin resistance had not had a regular cycle in over two years. Within four months, her cycle became predictable and her androgen markers improved on repeat bloodwork.

Hormonal Health
Lifestyle Improvement ⚡

From exhausted by noon to sustained energy all day

A client who had been relying on multiple cups of chai to get through the day. Her ferritin and B12 were both low. Four weeks after addressing these, she reported sustained energy for the first time in years.

Energy and Fatigue
Client Stories

What clients have said.

Free Assessment

How is your body really doing?

Answer 6 quick questions. Get your personalised health score plus a real insight into what your body is telling you right now.

Question 1 of 6

How would you describe your energy levels throughout the day?

💡 Did you know? Fatigue in 80% of cases traces back to a blood marker imbalance, specifically low ferritin, B12, or vitamin D, not a lack of sleep or motivation.
Question 2 of 6

How does your digestion feel most of the time?

💡 Did you know? Your gut produces 90% of your serotonin. Chronic bloating is not just uncomfortable. It directly affects your mood, immunity, and hormones.
Question 3 of 6

How is your sleep quality?

💡 Did you know? Poor sleep raises cortisol, which causes fat storage around the abdomen and disrupts blood sugar, even if your diet is perfect. Sleep is nutrition too.
Question 4 of 6

How would you describe your current eating pattern?

💡 Did you know? Your dahi, moong dal, and haldi contain some of the most powerful gut-healing compounds in the world. Most people just do not know how to use them correctly for their specific condition.
Question 5 of 6

Are you experiencing any of these symptoms regularly?

💡 Did you know? Hair fall, PCOS, and unexplained weight gain are frequently caused by the same root cause: insulin resistance. One imbalance can create 5 to 7 different symptoms simultaneously.
Question 6 of 6

How well are you managing stress in your daily life?

💡 Did you know? Cortisol depletes magnesium, B vitamins, and vitamin C every single day. Chronic stress is not just a mental health issue. It is a nutritional deficiency accelerator.

One last step!

Enter your name and WhatsApp number to receive your personalised health score and what your answers suggest about your body right now.

Book Your Consultation

Let's start with a conversation.

Sometimes all you need is someone to listen, understand what has been happening, and guide you in the right direction. This 30-minute consultation is designed to understand your health concerns and help us decide whether we are the right fit to work together.

30-Minute Introductory Consultation
₹499

A one-to-one online consultation with Tanvi Khedkar

During this consultation, we'll discuss
✓Your current health concerns
✓What you are hoping to achieve
✓What you have already tried
✓Your eating habits and lifestyle
✓Challenges that are making progress difficult
✓My approach and how I work with clients
✓Whether working together would be the right next step
This consultation is for you if...
✔You are tired of trying different diets without lasting results.
✔You want a personalised approach instead of generic advice.
✔You are dealing with digestive issues, PCOS, thyroid concerns, cholesterol, diabetes, or weight management.
✔You want to understand what is happening with your body before committing to a long-term programme.
What happens after the consultation?

If we both feel we are a good fit, I will recommend the most suitable way to move forward based on your goals and health concerns.

There is absolutely no obligation to join a programme after the consultation.

Tanvi Khedkar

Before your appointment

You do not need to prepare anything special. Simply come with:

·An open mind
·Your health concerns
·Any questions you would like to ask

If you have had recent blood tests and would like to discuss them, you are welcome to keep them handy. They are not required for this consultation.
